title: hedypnois rhagadioloides subsp. tubaeformis
intro: Hedypnois rhagadioloides subsp. tubaeformis, also known as the tuberous heliotrope, is a flowering plant species within the family Compositae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The tuberous heliotrope has been found to have health benefits including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
